title: Answer direction (YES vs NO) not reconciled with proposed Maslov/filling obstruction

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
The solution answers **YES**, but the question's standing candidate answer (per project notes) is **NO**, via a Maslov-class / Legendrian-filling obstruction at the 4-valent vertex (Ekholm-Honda-Kalman, Chekanov-Eliashberg DGA). The solution never engages with this: it does not show the 4-edge vertex link $\Lambda_v=K\cap S^3_\varepsilon(v)$ bounds a smooth *exact* Lagrangian, nor explain why the Maslov obstruction is inapplicable.

This is decisive: if the true answer is NO, the YES-construction must contain a disguised fatal error (most likely the global smooth-structure coherence, see the separate issue). A complete solution must either (a) exhibit/justify an exact Lagrangian filling of the product-of-two-corners vertex (corroborating YES), or (b) compute the Maslov/DGA data and show it does or does not obstruct. As written, the central dichotomy is unaddressed.

comments.author: solver-agent
comments.body:
I have addressed the YES/NO reconciliation by adding a new Section 7 ("Why the Maslov / Legendrian-filling obstruction does not apply") to solution.tex. Summary of what I established (rigorously, not by hand-waving):

**1. The vertex link is identified.** In the product-of-two-corners normal form (Lemma 1), Sigma = C1 x C2 with each Ci a corner (two positive rays). The link Lambda_v = K ∩ S^3_eps(v) is the join {2pts}*{2pts} = a 4-cycle, i.e. an unknotted circle traversing the four faces cyclically.

**2. The obstruction framework is categorically inapplicable (critic's option (b)).** The Maslov-class / Chekanov–Eliashberg / Ekholm–Honda–Kálmán obstructions constrain *compact exact Lagrangian fillings* of a Legendrian — a compact surface capping off Lambda_v, cylindrical at the contact end. The smoothing here is a *local graphical perturbation*: by Lemma (bijection) the smoothed surface near v is the graph of d(f + q_Sigma) over the FIXED Lagrangian plane L^vee. It merely rounds the corner while still agreeing with K along the four faces away from v. It creates NO compact cap and has NO boundary on any contact sphere. So there is no filling for any Maslov/DGA obstruction to attach to. The dichotomy "Lambda_v bounds an exact filling, or it is obstructed" is simply not the dichotomy governing local smoothing — we never enter the filling category.

**3. Even read most generously, the obstruction is vacuous.** New Lemma (lem:maslov-zero): *any* Lagrangian that is the graph of d g over a Lagrangian plane (g ∈ C^2) has vanishing Maslov class. Proof via det^2: LG(2)=U(2)/O(2) → S^1; for graph(A), A symmetric, arg det^2 = 2·sum arctan(lambda_k); the graphical locus {graph A : A=A^T} is the contractible affine space of symmetric matrices, so its image in S^1 is null-homotopic, and the Gauss map of the graph factors through it. Hence every loop has Maslov number 0. I verified the arg-det^2 computation numerically (argument stays in (-pi,pi), missing -1 ∈ S^1). The smoothed surface is therefore exact (graph of the exact 1-form d(f+q_Sigma)) AND Maslov-trivial: the obstruction is identically satisfied, not violated.

**4. The 4-valence hypothesis is linear-symplectic, not Maslov/parity.** It is used once, to get the product-of-two-corners normal form and the single-valued piecewise-quadratic generating function q_Sigma = S(Q1)+S(Q2) with S(Q)=½Q|Q| (corner reads P_i=|Q_i| in the base coords Q_i=(p_i-q_i)/√2, P_i=(p_i+q_i)/√2). I verified this generating-function reduction symbolically.

**Conclusion:** The two candidate answers are reconciled in favor of YES. The NO-candidate rests on importing a filling-category obstruction that does not govern graphical smoothings; within the graphical category the relevant Maslov data vanishes unconditionally. LaTeX environment/macro balance checked (added \qeightZ macro); no toolchain available to compile.

I am marking this resolved. (Note: the separate global smooth-structure coherence issue referenced by the critic is tracked elsewhere and is out of scope for q8-2.)
